summary.overall: Chapter 5 item bank is broadly faithful to FAA JO 7110.65BB source text but systemic formatting defects degrade educational effectiveness. Approximately 60% of items (286/473) require remediation, primarily for paragraph-location scaffolding, answer-position bias, and answer-length cues. Source fidelity is high with one factual error found. Cross-format coverage exists for most paragraphs.
patterns: Paragraph-location scaffolding in 123 stems/fronts teaches document navigation instead of operational substance
Answer-position bias in 185 multiple-choice items places correct answer consistently first
Answer-length cues in 89 items make correct answer conspicuously longer than distractors
Negative-stem overuse in 34 items where exclusion recognition is not the tested skill
Thin explanations in 6 items restate answer without teaching controlling rule
Overloaded flashcard backs (4 items) contain too many retrieval targets
Context-light flashcard fronts (3 items) lack sufficient concept identification
One factual error: flashcard miscounts paragraph references
One malformed reverse card references paragraph numbers on back
generation_guidance: Eliminate paragraph references from all learner-facing text; store para_id in metadata only
Randomize answer order so correct answer is never consistently first across items
Match distractor length and specificity to correct-answer detail level
Limit flashcards to one retrieval target; split lists into individual cards
Write explanations that teach the operational principle, not restate the answer
Use negative stems only when exclusion recognition is the genuine operational skill
Prefer operational-decision stems (situation -> controller action) over verbatim recall
Validate every reverse card for answerability in both directions
Ensure flashcard fronts identify the concept domain without needing the back
Fill cross-format gaps where paragraphs lack coverage in one or more learning modes
